context.pr_title: fix: repair window size in macOS
context.pr_body:
When a program is running on macOS with an external monitor connected, the values obtained through 
`glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)`
will be larger. 
It is more appropriate to use 
`glfw.get_window_size(self.win)`
to retrieve the logical size.

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt: You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README # Animated Drawings ![Sequence 02](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6675724/219223438-2c93f9cb-d4b5-45e9-a433-149ed76affa6.gif) This repo contains an implementation of the algorithm described in the paper, `A Method for Animating Children's Drawings of the Human Figure' (to appear in Transactions on Graphics and to be presented at SIGGRAPH 2023). In addition, this repo aims to be a useful creative tool in its own right, allowing you to flexibly create animations starring your own drawn characters. If you do create something fun with this, let us know! Use hashtag **#FAIRAnimatedDrawings**, or tag me on twitter: [@hjessmith](https://twitter.com/hjessmith/). Project website: [http://www.fairanimateddrawings.com](http://www.fairanimateddrawings.com) Video overview of [Animated Drawings OS Project](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WsMUKQLVsOI) ## Installation *This project has been tested with macOS Ventura 13.2.1 and Ubuntu 18.04. If you're installing on another operating system, you may encounter issues.* We *strongly* recommend activating a Python virtual environment prior to installing Animated Drawings. Conda's Miniconda is a great choice. Follow [these steps](https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/stable/user-guide/install/index.html) to download and install it. Then run the following commands: ````bash # create and activate the virtual environment conda create --name animated_drawings python=3.8.13 conda activate animated_drawings # clone AnimatedDrawings and use pip to install git clone https://github.com/facebookresearch/AnimatedDrawings.git cd AnimatedDrawings pip install -e . ```` Mac M1/M2 users: if you get architecture errors, make sure your `~/.condarc` does not have `osx-64`, but only `osx-arm64` and `noarch` in its subdirs listing. You can see that it's going to go sideways as early as `conda create` because it will show `osx-64` instead of `osx-arm64` versions of libraries under "The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED". ## Using Animated Drawings ### Quick Start Now that everything's set up, let's animate some drawings! To get started, follow these steps: 1. Open a terminal and activate the animated_drawings conda environment: ````bash ~ % conda activate animated_drawings ```` 2. Ensure you're in the root directory of AnimatedDrawings: ````bash (animated_drawings) ~ % cd {location of AnimatedDrawings on your computer} ```` 3. Start up a Python interpreter: ````bash (animated_drawings) AnimatedDrawings % python ```` 4. Copy and paste the follow two lines into the interpreter: ````python from animated_drawings import render render.start('./examples/config/mvc/interactive_window_example.yaml') ```` If everything is installed correctly, an interactive window should appear on your screen. (Use spacebar to pause/unpause the scene, arrow keys to move back and forth in time, and q to close the screen.)

When a program is running on macOS with an external monitor connected, the values obtained through

arguments.reason: Identify usage of glfw.get_framebuffer_size and related window sizing code
arguments.expected_information: Locations of get_framebuffer_size and related window sizing logic in window management

tool_calls.raw_result:
# Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
# This source code is licensed under the MIT license found in the
# LICENSE file in the root directory of this source tree.

from animated_drawings.view.view import View
from animated_drawings.view.shaders.shader import Shader
from animated_drawings.view.utils import get_projection_matrix
from animated_drawings.utils import read_background_image
from animated_drawings.model.scene import Scene
from animated_drawings.model.camera import Camera
from animated_drawings.model.transform import Transform
from animated_drawings.config import ViewConfig
import glfw
import OpenGL.GL as GL
import logging
from typing import Tuple, Dict
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt
from pathlib import Path
from pkg_resources import resource_filename


class WindowView(View):
    """Window View for rendering into a visible window"""

    def __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) -> None:
        super().__init__(cfg)

        glfw.init()

        self.camera: Camera = Camera(cfg.camera_pos, cfg.camera_fwd)

        self.win: glfw._GLFWwindow
        self._create_window(*cfg.window_dimensions)  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        self.shaders: Dict[str, Shader] = {}
        self.shader_ids: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._prep_shaders()

        self.fboId: GL.GLint
        self._prep_background_image()

        self._set_shader_projections(get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()))

    def _prep_background_image(self) -> None:
        """ Initialize framebuffer object for background image, if specified. """

        # if nothing specified, return
        if not self.cfg.background_image:
            return

        # load background image
        _txtr: npt.NDArray[np.uint8] = read_background_image(self.cfg.background_image)

        # create the opengl texture and send it data
        self.txtr_h, self.txtr_w, _ = _txtr.shape
        self.txtr_id = GL.glGenTextures(1)
        GL.glPixelStorei(GL.GL_UNPACK_ALIGNMENT, 4)
        GL.glBindTexture(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_BASE_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_MAX_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexImage2D(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, GL.GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, _txtr)

        # make framebuffer object
        self.fboId: GL.GLint = GL.glGenFramebuffers(1)
        GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
        GL.glFramebufferTexture2D(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, GL.GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id, 0)

    def _prep_shaders(self) -> None:
        BVH_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.vert"))
        BVH_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('bvh_shader', str(BVH_VERT), str(BVH_FRAG))

        COLOR_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.vert"))
        COLOR_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('color_shader', str(COLOR_VERT), str(COLOR_FRAG))

        TEXTURE_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.vert"))
        TEXTURE_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('texture_shader', str(TEXTURE_VERT), str(TEXTURE_FRAG), texture=True)

    def _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None:
        try:
            view_transform: npt.NDArray[np.float32] = np.linalg.inv(camera.get_world_transform())
        except Exception as e:
            msg = f'Error inverting camera world transform: {e}'
            logging.critical(msg)
            assert False, msg

        for shader_name in self.shaders:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            view_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(self.shader_ids[shader_name], "view")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(view_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, view_transform.T)

    def _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None:
        for shader_id in self.shader_ids.values():
            GL.glUseProgram(shader_id)
            proj_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(shader_id, "proj")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(proj_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, proj_m.T)

    def _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None:
        self.shaders[shader_name] = Shader(vert_path, frag_path)
        self.shader_ids[shader_name] = self.shaders[shader_name].glid  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        if 'texture' in kwargs and kwargs['texture'] is True:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            GL.glUniform1i(GL.glGetUniformLocation(
                self.shader_ids[shader_name], 'texture0'), 0)

    def _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None:

        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MAJOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MINOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_FORWARD_COMPAT, GL.GL_TRUE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_PROFILE, glfw.OPENGL_CORE_PROFILE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.RESIZABLE, False)

        self.win = glfw.create_window(width, height, 'Viewer', None, None)

        glfw.make_context_current(self.win)

        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_CULL_FACE)
        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_DEPTH_TEST)
        GL.glClearColor(*self.cfg.clear_color)

        logging.info(f'OpenGL Version: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'GLSL: { GL.glGetString(GL.GL_SHADING_LANGUAGE_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'Renderer: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_RENDERER).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

    def set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None:
        self.scene = scene

    def render(self, scene: Transform) -> None:
        GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size())

        # draw the background image if exists
        if self.cfg.background_image:
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_DRAW_FRAMEBUFFER, 0)
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
            win_w, win_h = self.get_framebuffer_size()
            GL.glBlitFramebuffer(0, 0, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, 0, win_w, win_h, GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT, GL.GL_LINEAR)

        self._update_shaders_view_transform(self.camera)

        scene.draw(shader_ids=self.shader_ids, viewer_cfg=self.cfg)

    def get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """ Return (width, height) of view's window. """
        return glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)

    def swap_buffers(self) -> None:
        glfw.swap_buffers(self.win)

    def clear_window(self) -> None:
        GL.glClear(GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL.GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT)  # type: ignore

    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """ Destroy the window when it's no longer being used. """
        glfw.destroy_window(self.win)
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
WindowView is a GLFW/OpenGL-based view that creates a window, manages a camera and shaders, and renders a Scene. It also supports an optional background image rendered via a framebuffer object.

### Architecture
- WindowView inherits from View and coordinates the rendering pipeline.
- It initializes a GLFW window, sets up OpenGL state, loads/initializes shaders, and prepares an optional background image as a texture attached to a framebuffer.
- It maintains a Camera for view transforms and uses projection matrices derived from the window's framebuffer size.
- Rendering flow: update view/projection uniforms across shaders, optionally blit a background image, and draw the scene.
- Window sizing is controlled at creation (RESIZABLE is False) but the code queries the framebuffer size to configure viewports and projections.

### Functions & Classes
- WindowView — Class: WindowView(View) — Manages a visible rendering window, camera, shaders, and optional background image for the animated drawings viewer.

- __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) — Initializes GLFW, creates the window using cfg.window_dimensions, sets up the camera, shaders, background image framebuffer (if any), and initial projection matrices based on the framebuffer size.

- _prep_background_image(self) -> None — Initializes framebuffer object for the background image if configured. Loads the image, creates an OpenGL texture, and attaches it to a framebuffer for later blitting into the window.

- _prep_shaders(self) -> None — Loads and compiles shaders for bvh_shader, color_shader, and texture_shader, storing shader objects and their GL program IDs. This is central to rendering various visual aspects (scene, colors, textures).

- _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None — Computes the view matrix from the camera’s world transform and updates the view uniform in all shaders. This keeps the rendered scene in sync with the camera.

- _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None — Uploads the projection matrix to all shader programs.

- _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None — Creates a Shader instance for the given vertex/fragment paths, stores it, and if texture=True is passed, configures the texture uniform in the shader. This centralizes shader creation and optional texture setup.

- _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None — Configures GLFW context hints (OpenGL 3.3 core, forward-compatible, non-resizable), creates the window, makes its context current, enables depth/culling, sets clear color, and logs OpenGL/GLSL/Renderer info. This is the primary window setup routine.

- set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None — Stores the scene to be rendered.

- render(self, scene: Transform) -> None — Performs rendering for a frame: sets the viewport to the framebuffer size, optionally blits the background image from the FBO to the window, updates the shader view transform, and draws the scene using the available shader programs.

- get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int] — Returns the current window framebuffer size via glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win). Key for the agent to locate where framebuffer sizing is obtained.

- swap_buffers(self) -> None — Swaps the front and back buffers for the window (displaying the rendered frame).

- clear_window(self) -> None — Clears color and depth buffers to prepare for a new frame.

- cleanup(self) -> None — Destroys the GLFW window when rendering is finished.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- get_framebuffer_size usage: get_framebuffer_size is implemented and used in multiple critical places:
  - __init__: to compute the initial projection matrix via get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()).
  - render: to set the viewport with GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size()) and to determine blitting dimensions when a background image is present.
- Window sizing logic: _create_window configures the window to be non-resizable (glfw.RESIZABLE, False), and the agent can trace how the code relies on a fixed window size for the projection and viewport setup. The code also uses the framebuffer size to drive shader uniform updates and the final render pass.
- Overall, the file contains the main window management, framebuffer size handling, and the rendering pipeline required to locate and modify get_framebuffer_size usage and related sizing logic.

4. Highlight the parts most relevant to the agent's stated goal.

summarization.user_prompt:
## File: animated_drawings/view/window_view.py

## Agent's reason for reading this file:
Identify usage of glfw.get_framebuffer_size and related window sizing code

## What the agent expects to find:
Locations of get_framebuffer_size and related window sizing logic in window management

## Source Code:
# Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
# This source code is licensed under the MIT license found in the
# LICENSE file in the root directory of this source tree.

from animated_drawings.view.view import View
from animated_drawings.view.shaders.shader import Shader
from animated_drawings.view.utils import get_projection_matrix
from animated_drawings.utils import read_background_image
from animated_drawings.model.scene import Scene
from animated_drawings.model.camera import Camera
from animated_drawings.model.transform import Transform
from animated_drawings.config import ViewConfig
import glfw
import OpenGL.GL as GL
import logging
from typing import Tuple, Dict
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt
from pathlib import Path
from pkg_resources import resource_filename


class WindowView(View):
    """Window View for rendering into a visible window"""

    def __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) -> None:
        super().__init__(cfg)

        glfw.init()

        self.camera: Camera = Camera(cfg.camera_pos, cfg.camera_fwd)

        self.win: glfw._GLFWwindow
        self._create_window(*cfg.window_dimensions)  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        self.shaders: Dict[str, Shader] = {}
        self.shader_ids: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._prep_shaders()

        self.fboId: GL.GLint
        self._prep_background_image()

        self._set_shader_projections(get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()))

    def _prep_background_image(self) -> None:
        """ Initialize framebuffer object for background image, if specified. """

        # if nothing specified, return
        if not self.cfg.background_image:
            return

        # load background image
        _txtr: npt.NDArray[np.uint8] = read_background_image(self.cfg.background_image)

        # create the opengl texture and send it data
        self.txtr_h, self.txtr_w, _ = _txtr.shape
        self.txtr_id = GL.glGenTextures(1)
        GL.glPixelStorei(GL.GL_UNPACK_ALIGNMENT, 4)
        GL.glBindTexture(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_BASE_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_MAX_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexImage2D(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, GL.GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, _txtr)

        # make framebuffer object
        self.fboId: GL.GLint = GL.glGenFramebuffers(1)
        GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
        GL.glFramebufferTexture2D(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, GL.GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id, 0)

    def _prep_shaders(self) -> None:
        BVH_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.vert"))
        BVH_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('bvh_shader', str(BVH_VERT), str(BVH_FRAG))

        COLOR_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.vert"))
        COLOR_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('color_shader', str(COLOR_VERT), str(COLOR_FRAG))

        TEXTURE_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.vert"))
        TEXTURE_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('texture_shader', str(TEXTURE_VERT), str(TEXTURE_FRAG), texture=True)

    def _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None:
        try:
            view_transform: npt.NDArray[np.float32] = np.linalg.inv(camera.get_world_transform())
        except Exception as e:
            msg = f'Error inverting camera world transform: {e}'
            logging.critical(msg)
            assert False, msg

        for shader_name in self.shaders:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            view_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(self.shader_ids[shader_name], "view")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(view_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, view_transform.T)

    def _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None:
        for shader_id in self.shader_ids.values():
            GL.glUseProgram(shader_id)
            proj_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(shader_id, "proj")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(proj_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, proj_m.T)

    def _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None:
        self.shaders[shader_name] = Shader(vert_path, frag_path)
        self.shader_ids[shader_name] = self.shaders[shader_name].glid  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        if 'texture' in kwargs and kwargs['texture'] is True:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            GL.glUniform1i(GL.glGetUniformLocation(
                self.shader_ids[shader_name], 'texture0'), 0)

    def _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None:

        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MAJOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MINOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_FORWARD_COMPAT, GL.GL_TRUE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_PROFILE, glfw.OPENGL_CORE_PROFILE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.RESIZABLE, False)

        self.win = glfw.create_window(width, height, 'Viewer', None, None)

        glfw.make_context_current(self.win)

        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_CULL_FACE)
        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_DEPTH_TEST)
        GL.glClearColor(*self.cfg.clear_color)

        logging.info(f'OpenGL Version: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'GLSL: { GL.glGetString(GL.GL_SHADING_LANGUAGE_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'Renderer: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_RENDERER).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

    def set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None:
        self.scene = scene

    def render(self, scene: Transform) -> None:
        GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size())

        # draw the background image if exists
        if self.cfg.background_image:
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_DRAW_FRAMEBUFFER, 0)
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
            win_w, win_h = self.get_framebuffer_size()
            GL.glBlitFramebuffer(0, 0, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, 0, win_w, win_h, GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT, GL.GL_LINEAR)

        self._update_shaders_view_transform(self.camera)

        scene.draw(shader_ids=self.shader_ids, viewer_cfg=self.cfg)

    def get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """ Return (width, height) of view's window. """
        return glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)

    def swap_buffers(self) -> None:
        glfw.swap_buffers(self.win)

    def clear_window(self) -> None:
        GL.glClear(GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL.GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT)  # type: ignore

    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """ Destroy the window when it's no longer being used. """
        glfw.destroy_window(self.win)
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
WindowView is a GLFW/OpenGL-based view that creates a window, manages a camera and shaders, and renders a Scene. It also supports an optional background image rendered via a framebuffer object.

### Architecture
- WindowView inherits from View and coordinates the rendering pipeline.
- It initializes a GLFW window, sets up OpenGL state, loads/initializes shaders, and prepares an optional background image as a texture attached to a framebuffer.
- It maintains a Camera for view transforms and uses projection matrices derived from the window's framebuffer size.
- Rendering flow: update view/projection uniforms across shaders, optionally blit a background image, and draw the scene.
- Window sizing is controlled at creation (RESIZABLE is False) but the code queries the framebuffer size to configure viewports and projections.

### Functions & Classes
- WindowView — Class: WindowView(View) — Manages a visible rendering window, camera, shaders, and optional background image for the animated drawings viewer.

- __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) — Initializes GLFW, creates the window using cfg.window_dimensions, sets up the camera, shaders, background image framebuffer (if any), and initial projection matrices based on the framebuffer size.

- _prep_background_image(self) -> None — Initializes framebuffer object for the background image if configured. Loads the image, creates an OpenGL texture, and attaches it to a framebuffer for later blitting into the window.

- _prep_shaders(self) -> None — Loads and compiles shaders for bvh_shader, color_shader, and texture_shader, storing shader objects and their GL program IDs. This is central to rendering various visual aspects (scene, colors, textures).

- _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None — Computes the view matrix from the camera’s world transform and updates the view uniform in all shaders. This keeps the rendered scene in sync with the camera.

- _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None — Uploads the projection matrix to all shader programs.

- _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None — Creates a Shader instance for the given vertex/fragment paths, stores it, and if texture=True is passed, configures the texture uniform in the shader. This centralizes shader creation and optional texture setup.

- _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None — Configures GLFW context hints (OpenGL 3.3 core, forward-compatible, non-resizable), creates the window, makes its context current, enables depth/culling, sets clear color, and logs OpenGL/GLSL/Renderer info. This is the primary window setup routine.

- set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None — Stores the scene to be rendered.

- render(self, scene: Transform) -> None — Performs rendering for a frame: sets the viewport to the framebuffer size, optionally blits the background image from the FBO to the window, updates the shader view transform, and draws the scene using the available shader programs.

- get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int] — Returns the current window framebuffer size via glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win). Key for the agent to locate where framebuffer sizing is obtained.

- swap_buffers(self) -> None — Swaps the front and back buffers for the window (displaying the rendered frame).

- clear_window(self) -> None — Clears color and depth buffers to prepare for a new frame.

- cleanup(self) -> None — Destroys the GLFW window when rendering is finished.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- get_framebuffer_size usage: get_framebuffer_size is implemented and used in multiple critical places:
  - __init__: to compute the initial projection matrix via get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()).
  - render: to set the viewport with GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size()) and to determine blitting dimensions when a background image is present.
- Window sizing logic: _create_window configures the window to be non-resizable (glfw.RESIZABLE, False), and the agent can trace how the code relies on a fixed window size for the projection and viewport setup. The code also uses the framebuffer size to drive shader uniform updates and the final render pass.
- Overall, the file contains the main window management, framebuffer size handling, and the rendering pipeline required to locate and modify get_framebuffer_size usage and related sizing logic.

arguments.expected_information: Exact usage of glfw.get_framebuffer_size and related width/height computations

tool_calls.raw_result:
# Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
# This source code is licensed under the MIT license found in the
# LICENSE file in the root directory of this source tree.

from animated_drawings.view.view import View
from animated_drawings.view.shaders.shader import Shader
from animated_drawings.view.utils import get_projection_matrix
from animated_drawings.utils import read_background_image
from animated_drawings.model.scene import Scene
from animated_drawings.model.camera import Camera
from animated_drawings.model.transform import Transform
from animated_drawings.config import ViewConfig
import glfw
import OpenGL.GL as GL
import logging
from typing import Tuple, Dict
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt
from pathlib import Path
from pkg_resources import resource_filename


class WindowView(View):
    """Window View for rendering into a visible window"""

    def __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) -> None:
        super().__init__(cfg)

        glfw.init()

        self.camera: Camera = Camera(cfg.camera_pos, cfg.camera_fwd)

        self.win: glfw._GLFWwindow
        self._create_window(*cfg.window_dimensions)  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        self.shaders: Dict[str, Shader] = {}
        self.shader_ids: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._prep_shaders()

        self.fboId: GL.GLint
        self._prep_background_image()

        self._set_shader_projections(get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()))

    def _prep_background_image(self) -> None:
        """ Initialize framebuffer object for background image, if specified. """

        # if nothing specified, return
        if not self.cfg.background_image:
            return

        # load background image
        _txtr: npt.NDArray[np.uint8] = read_background_image(self.cfg.background_image)

        # create the opengl texture and send it data
        self.txtr_h, self.txtr_w, _ = _txtr.shape
        self.txtr_id = GL.glGenTextures(1)
        GL.glPixelStorei(GL.GL_UNPACK_ALIGNMENT, 4)
        GL.glBindTexture(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_BASE_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_MAX_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexImage2D(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, GL.GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, _txtr)

        # make framebuffer object
        self.fboId: GL.GLint = GL.glGenFramebuffers(1)
        GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
        GL.glFramebufferTexture2D(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, GL.GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id, 0)

    def _prep_shaders(self) -> None:
        BVH_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.vert"))
        BVH_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('bvh_shader', str(BVH_VERT), str(BVH_FRAG))

        COLOR_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.vert"))
        COLOR_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('color_shader', str(COLOR_VERT), str(COLOR_FRAG))

        TEXTURE_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.vert"))
        TEXTURE_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('texture_shader', str(TEXTURE_VERT), str(TEXTURE_FRAG), texture=True)

    def _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None:
        try:
            view_transform: npt.NDArray[np.float32] = np.linalg.inv(camera.get_world_transform())
        except Exception as e:
            msg = f'Error inverting camera world transform: {e}'
            logging.critical(msg)
            assert False, msg

        for shader_name in self.shaders:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            view_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(self.shader_ids[shader_name], "view")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(view_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, view_transform.T)

    def _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None:
        for shader_id in self.shader_ids.values():
            GL.glUseProgram(shader_id)
            proj_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(shader_id, "proj")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(proj_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, proj_m.T)

    def _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None:
        self.shaders[shader_name] = Shader(vert_path, frag_path)
        self.shader_ids[shader_name] = self.shaders[shader_name].glid  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        if 'texture' in kwargs and kwargs['texture'] is True:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            GL.glUniform1i(GL.glGetUniformLocation(
                self.shader_ids[shader_name], 'texture0'), 0)

    def _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None:

        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MAJOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MINOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_FORWARD_COMPAT, GL.GL_TRUE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_PROFILE, glfw.OPENGL_CORE_PROFILE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.RESIZABLE, False)

        self.win = glfw.create_window(width, height, 'Viewer', None, None)

        glfw.make_context_current(self.win)

        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_CULL_FACE)
        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_DEPTH_TEST)
        GL.glClearColor(*self.cfg.clear_color)

        logging.info(f'OpenGL Version: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'GLSL: { GL.glGetString(GL.GL_SHADING_LANGUAGE_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'Renderer: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_RENDERER).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

    def set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None:
        self.scene = scene

    def render(self, scene: Transform) -> None:
        GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size())

        # draw the background image if exists
        if self.cfg.background_image:
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_DRAW_FRAMEBUFFER, 0)
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
            win_w, win_h = self.get_framebuffer_size()
            GL.glBlitFramebuffer(0, 0, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, 0, win_w, win_h, GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT, GL.GL_LINEAR)

        self._update_shaders_view_transform(self.camera)

        scene.draw(shader_ids=self.shader_ids, viewer_cfg=self.cfg)

    def get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """ Return (width, height) of view's window. """
        return glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)

    def swap_buffers(self) -> None:
        glfw.swap_buffers(self.win)

    def clear_window(self) -> None:
        GL.glClear(GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL.GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT)  # type: ignore

    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """ Destroy the window when it's no longer being used. """
        glfw.destroy_window(self.win)
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
Defines WindowView, a GLFW/OpenGL-based View that renders a Scene into a visible window, including shader management and optional background image support. It also handles framebuffer sizing, projection setup, and rendering lifecycle.

### Architecture
- WindowView extends View and orchestrates the rendering pipeline for a windowed view.
- Core responsibilities:
  - Window creation and OpenGL context setup (_create_window).
  - Shader loading and management (_prep_shaders, _initiatize_shader).
  - Optional background image handling via a framebuffer object (_prep_background_image).
  - Camera/view/projection management (_update_shaders_view_transform, _set_shader_projections).
  - Rendering lifecycle (render, clear_window, swap_buffers, cleanup).
- Interacts with:
  - Camera to compute view transforms.
  - Scene to draw objects with the prepared shaders.
  - read_background_image for optional background texture.
  - get_projection_matrix to build the initial projection from the framebuffer size.
  - Shader class to compile/use GLSL programs.

### Functions & Classes

- WindowView — Window View for rendering into a visible window.  
  Primary class that coordinates windowed rendering, shader management, and optional background imagery.

- __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) -> None — Initialize the window view:
  - Creates the GLFW context and window, initializes the camera, shader programs, and optional background image, and sets the initial projection based on the current framebuffer size.
  - Importance: Establishes the full rendering pipeline and ensures projection and view matrices are prepared before rendering starts.

- _prep_background_image(self) -> None — Prepare background framebuffer/image texture if configured:
  - Loads background image, creates an OpenGL texture, and attaches it to a read framebuffer for later blitting.
  - Importance: Enables a background image to be composited behind the rendered scene when configured.

- _prep_shaders(self) -> None — Load and initialize shaders:
  - Loads BVH, COLOR, and TEXTURE shader programs from resource files and stores them; enables texture shader with an appropriate uniform if used.
  - Importance: Sets up the multiple shader programs used for rendering various aspects of the scene and background.

- _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None — Update shader view matrices:
  - Computes the inverse of the camera world transform and uploads it as the view matrix to all shaders.
  - Importance: Keeps the rendered scene aligned with the current camera pose.

- _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None — Update shader projection matrices:
  - Uploads the projection matrix (transposed) to all shader programs.
  - Importance: Establishes the projection (perspective) used for rendering from the current window framebuffer size.

- _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None — Create and configure a shader:
  - Instantiates a Shader, stores it and its GL program ID; if texture is requested, sets the texture uniform to texture unit 0.
  - Importance: Centralizes shader creation and optional texture setup for consistent usage.

- _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None — Configure and create GLFW window:
  - Sets OpenGL version/profile hints, creates the window, makes the context current, enables culling/depth test, and logs GL info.
  - Importance: Establishes the rendering surface and context that all subsequent rendering relies on.

- set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None — Assign the scene to render:
  - Stores the provided scene for use during render.
  - Importance: Connects the WindowView to the scene graph or content to draw.

- render(self, scene: Transform) -> None — Render a frame:
  - Sets the viewport to the current framebuffer size.
  - If a background image exists, blits it from the background framebuffer to the window framebuffer.
  - Updates the view transform from the current camera and delegates drawing to the scene with the prepared shader programs.
  - Importance: Core rendering loop for a frame; handles both background compositing and scene drawing.

- get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int] — Return the window’s framebuffer size:
  - Returns (width, height) via glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win).
  - Importance: Central source of window dimensions used for projection setup and viewport configuration.

- swap_buffers(self) -> None — Swap the front and back buffers:
  - Calls glfw.swap_buffers(self.win).
  - Importance: Presents the rendered frame to the display.

- clear_window(self) -> None — Clear buffers for a new frame:
  - Clears color and depth buffers.
  - Importance: Prepares a clean slate for rendering the new frame.

- cleanup(self) -> None — Destroy the window and clean up:
  - Destroys the GLFW window.
  - Importance: Resource cleanup when the view is no longer needed.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- Primary focus is on get_framebuffer_size usage details:
  - get_framebuffer_size is defined as returning glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win) and is used in:
    - __init__: get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()) to initialize the projection.
    - _set_shader_projections: passes the framebuffer-derived projection to all shaders.
    - render: GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size()) to set the viewport for the current framebuffer.
    - render: win_w, win_h = self.get_framebuffer_size() to determine blit destination dimensions when compositing the background image.
  - These usages show how width/height are derived from the actual framebuffer backing the window and propagate through projection setup, viewport configuration, and background compositing.
- Identifying these call sites helps the agent understand where framebuffer size impacts rendering and what to adjust if the window dimensions or projection behavior need modification (e.g., handling resize, changing projection math, or ensuring consistent texture/blit dimensions).

from animated_drawings.view.utils import get_projection_matrix
from animated_drawings.utils import read_background_image
from animated_drawings.model.scene import Scene
from animated_drawings.model.camera import Camera
from animated_drawings.model.transform import Transform
from animated_drawings.config import ViewConfig
import glfw
import OpenGL.GL as GL
import logging
from typing import Tuple, Dict
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt
from pathlib import Path
from pkg_resources import resource_filename

    """Window View for rendering into a visible window"""

        self._prep_background_image()

        self._set_shader_projections(get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()))

    def _prep_background_image(self) -> None:

            return

        # create the opengl texture and send it data

        self.txtr_id = GL.glGenTextures(1)
        GL.glPixelStorei(GL.GL_UNPACK_ALIGNMENT, 4)
        GL.glBindTexture(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_BASE_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexParameteri(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL.GL_TEXTURE_MAX_LEVEL, 0)
        GL.glTexImage2D(GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, GL.GL_RGBA, GL.GL_UNSIGNED_BYTE, _txtr)

        # make framebuffer object
        self.fboId: GL.GLint = GL.glGenFramebuffers(1)

        GL.glFramebufferTexture2D(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, GL.GL_COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, GL.GL_TEXTURE_2D, self.txtr_id, 0)

    def _prep_shaders(self) -> None:
        BVH_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.vert"))
        BVH_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/bvh.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('bvh_shader', str(BVH_VERT), str(BVH_FRAG))

        COLOR_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.vert"))
        COLOR_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/color.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('color_shader', str(COLOR_VERT), str(COLOR_FRAG))

        TEXTURE_VERT = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.vert"))
        TEXTURE_FRAG = Path(resource_filename(__name__, "shaders/texture.frag"))
        self._initiatize_shader('texture_shader', str(TEXTURE_VERT), str(TEXTURE_FRAG), texture=True)

    def _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None:
        try:
            view_transform: npt.NDArray[np.float32] = np.linalg.inv(camera.get_world_transform())
        except Exception as e:
            msg = f'Error inverting camera world transform: {e}'
            logging.critical(msg)
            assert False, msg

        for shader_name in self.shaders:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            view_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(self.shader_ids[shader_name], "view")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(view_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, view_transform.T)

    def _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None:
        for shader_id in self.shader_ids.values():
            GL.glUseProgram(shader_id)
            proj_loc = GL.glGetUniformLocation(shader_id, "proj")
            GL.glUniformMatrix4fv(proj_loc, 1, GL.GL_FALSE, proj_m.T)

    def _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None:
        self.shaders[shader_name] = Shader(vert_path, frag_path)
        self.shader_ids[shader_name] = self.shaders[shader_name].glid  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

        if 'texture' in kwargs and kwargs['texture'] is True:
            GL.glUseProgram(self.shader_ids[shader_name])
            GL.glUniform1i(GL.glGetUniformLocation(
                self.shader_ids[shader_name], 'texture0'), 0)

    def _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None:

        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MAJOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.CONTEXT_VERSION_MINOR, 3)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_FORWARD_COMPAT, GL.GL_TRUE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.OPENGL_PROFILE, glfw.OPENGL_CORE_PROFILE)
        glfw.window_hint(glfw.RESIZABLE, False)

        self.win = glfw.create_window(width, height, 'Viewer', None, None)

        glfw.make_context_current(self.win)

        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_CULL_FACE)
        GL.glEnable(GL.GL_DEPTH_TEST)
        GL.glClearColor(*self.cfg.clear_color)

        logging.info(f'OpenGL Version: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'GLSL: { GL.glGetString(GL.GL_SHADING_LANGUAGE_VERSION).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]
        logging.info(f'Renderer: {GL.glGetString(GL.GL_RENDERER).decode()}')  # pyright: ignore[reportGeneralTypeIssues]

    def set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None:
        self.scene = scene

    def render(self, scene: Transform) -> None:
        GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size())

        # draw the background image if exists
        if self.cfg.background_image:
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_DRAW_FRAMEBUFFER, 0)
            GL.glBindFramebuffer(GL.GL_READ_FRAMEBUFFER, self.fboId)
            win_w, win_h = self.get_framebuffer_size()
            GL.glBlitFramebuffer(0, 0, self.txtr_w, self.txtr_h, 0, 0, win_w, win_h, GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT, GL.GL_LINEAR)

        self._update_shaders_view_transform(self.camera)

        scene.draw(shader_ids=self.shader_ids, viewer_cfg=self.cfg)

    def get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """ Return (width, height) of view's window. """
        return glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)

    def swap_buffers(self) -> None:
        glfw.swap_buffers(self.win)

    def clear_window(self) -> None:
        GL.glClear(GL.GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT | GL.GL_DEPTH_BUFFER_BIT)  # type: ignore

    def cleanup(self) -> None:
        """ Destroy the window when it's no longer being used. """
        glfw.destroy_window(self.win)
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
Defines WindowView, a GLFW/OpenGL-based View that renders a Scene into a visible window, including shader management and optional background image support. It also handles framebuffer sizing, projection setup, and rendering lifecycle.

### Architecture
- WindowView extends View and orchestrates the rendering pipeline for a windowed view.
- Core responsibilities:
  - Window creation and OpenGL context setup (_create_window).
  - Shader loading and management (_prep_shaders, _initiatize_shader).
  - Optional background image handling via a framebuffer object (_prep_background_image).
  - Camera/view/projection management (_update_shaders_view_transform, _set_shader_projections).
  - Rendering lifecycle (render, clear_window, swap_buffers, cleanup).
- Interacts with:
  - Camera to compute view transforms.
  - Scene to draw objects with the prepared shaders.
  - read_background_image for optional background texture.
  - get_projection_matrix to build the initial projection from the framebuffer size.
  - Shader class to compile/use GLSL programs.

### Functions & Classes

- WindowView — Window View for rendering into a visible window.  
  Primary class that coordinates windowed rendering, shader management, and optional background imagery.

- __init__(self, cfg: ViewConfig) -> None — Initialize the window view:
  - Creates the GLFW context and window, initializes the camera, shader programs, and optional background image, and sets the initial projection based on the current framebuffer size.
  - Importance: Establishes the full rendering pipeline and ensures projection and view matrices are prepared before rendering starts.

- _prep_background_image(self) -> None — Prepare background framebuffer/image texture if configured:
  - Loads background image, creates an OpenGL texture, and attaches it to a read framebuffer for later blitting.
  - Importance: Enables a background image to be composited behind the rendered scene when configured.

- _prep_shaders(self) -> None — Load and initialize shaders:
  - Loads BVH, COLOR, and TEXTURE shader programs from resource files and stores them; enables texture shader with an appropriate uniform if used.
  - Importance: Sets up the multiple shader programs used for rendering various aspects of the scene and background.

- _update_shaders_view_transform(self, camera: Camera) -> None — Update shader view matrices:
  - Computes the inverse of the camera world transform and uploads it as the view matrix to all shaders.
  - Importance: Keeps the rendered scene aligned with the current camera pose.

- _set_shader_projections(self, proj_m: npt.NDArray[np.float32]) -> None — Update shader projection matrices:
  - Uploads the projection matrix (transposed) to all shader programs.
  - Importance: Establishes the projection (perspective) used for rendering from the current window framebuffer size.

- _initiatize_shader(self, shader_name: str, vert_path: str, frag_path: str, **kwargs) -> None — Create and configure a shader:
  - Instantiates a Shader, stores it and its GL program ID; if texture is requested, sets the texture uniform to texture unit 0.
  - Importance: Centralizes shader creation and optional texture setup for consistent usage.

- _create_window(self, width: int, height: int) -> None — Configure and create GLFW window:
  - Sets OpenGL version/profile hints, creates the window, makes the context current, enables culling/depth test, and logs GL info.
  - Importance: Establishes the rendering surface and context that all subsequent rendering relies on.

- set_scene(self, scene: Scene) -> None — Assign the scene to render:
  - Stores the provided scene for use during render.
  - Importance: Connects the WindowView to the scene graph or content to draw.

- render(self, scene: Transform) -> None — Render a frame:
  - Sets the viewport to the current framebuffer size.
  - If a background image exists, blits it from the background framebuffer to the window framebuffer.
  - Updates the view transform from the current camera and delegates drawing to the scene with the prepared shader programs.
  - Importance: Core rendering loop for a frame; handles both background compositing and scene drawing.

- get_framebuffer_size(self) -> Tuple[int, int] — Return the window’s framebuffer size:
  - Returns (width, height) via glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win).
  - Importance: Central source of window dimensions used for projection setup and viewport configuration.

- swap_buffers(self) -> None — Swap the front and back buffers:
  - Calls glfw.swap_buffers(self.win).
  - Importance: Presents the rendered frame to the display.

- clear_window(self) -> None — Clear buffers for a new frame:
  - Clears color and depth buffers.
  - Importance: Prepares a clean slate for rendering the new frame.

- cleanup(self) -> None — Destroy the window and clean up:
  - Destroys the GLFW window.
  - Importance: Resource cleanup when the view is no longer needed.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- Primary focus is on get_framebuffer_size usage details:
  - get_framebuffer_size is defined as returning glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win) and is used in:
    - __init__: get_projection_matrix(*self.get_framebuffer_size()) to initialize the projection.
    - _set_shader_projections: passes the framebuffer-derived projection to all shaders.
    - render: GL.glViewport(0, 0, *self.get_framebuffer_size()) to set the viewport for the current framebuffer.
    - render: win_w, win_h = self.get_framebuffer_size() to determine blit destination dimensions when compositing the background image.
  - These usages show how width/height are derived from the actual framebuffer backing the window and propagate through projection setup, viewport configuration, and background compositing.
- Identifying these call sites helps the agent understand where framebuffer size impacts rendering and what to adjust if the window dimensions or projection behavior need modification (e.g., handling resize, changing projection math, or ensuring consistent texture/blit dimensions).

output.pr_title: fix: repair window size in macOS
output.pr_body:
MacOS can report a framebuffer size larger than the logical window size when an external monitor is connected. The rendering and projection setup should rely on the logical window size rather than the framebuffer size to avoid oversizing or misaligned rendering. This PR updates the size source used for projection and viewport calculations to use glfw.get_window_size(self.win) instead of glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win). The change is localized to the WindowView class, preserving the existing API surface and call sites.

Affected area:
- animated_drawings/view/window_view.py: WindowView.get_framebuffer_size()
  - This method is the single source for the current window size used to initialize projection matrices and to set the viewport during rendering. By switching to the window size, the logical dimensions become the basis for rendering calculations, which aligns with the intended behavior on macOS when a monitor is connected.

No changes are required to the call sites since they continue to use the existing get_framebuffer_size() method, which will now return the logical size.
output.masca_optimized:
MASCA optimization focused on the specific PR:
- Objective: Ensure rendering uses the logical window size on macOS by switching from framebuffer size to window size where applicable.
- Scope: Narrow change to the WindowView.size retrieval logic to affect projection initialization and viewport setup without altering public APIs.
- Targeted changes:
  - File: animated_drawings/view/window_view.py
  - Function: WindowView.get_framebuffer_size
  - Change: Replace glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win) with glfw.get_window_size(self.win)
- Rationale: get_framebuffer_size returns pixels that may be scaled on macOS with external displays, whereas get_window_size returns the logical size used for layout and projection. This ensures consistent rendering dimensions across setups.
- Impact assessment:
  - Projections: Initial projection and subsequent updates will be computed using the logical size, potentially changing the aspect ratio if the framebuffer was significantly larger. This aligns with the intended behavior described in the PR.
  - Rendering: Viewport size and any background blitting will use the logical window size, avoiding over-sizing artifacts on macOS.
- Validation plan:
  - Run the application and verify that resizing (if allowed) or initial sizing uses the expected logical dimensions.
  - Ensure tests that rely on get_framebuffer_size() semantics still pass, now indirectly returning the logical size.
  - Confirm rendering looks correct on macOS with external monitors.

files_to_modify.reason: Change the source of the window size from framebuffer size to logical window size to fix rendering sizing issues on macOS with external monitors.

functions_to_modify.reason: Currently returns the framebuffer size; should return the logical window size to reflect the correct rendering dimensions on macOS. This aligns with the PR's intent to use glfw.get_window_size(self.win).

output.analysis_summary: The PR describes a sizing discrepancy on macOS when an external monitor is used, where glfw.get_framebuffer_size returns a larger size than the logical window. The rendering pipeline relies on these dimensions for projection and viewport setup. The targeted change is to modify the WindowView.get_framebuffer_size method to return glfw.get_window_size(self.win) instead of glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win). This preserves the current API surface while correcting the size source used across initial projection calculation, viewport configuration, and background compositing. The modification is minimal, localized to one method, and should resolve macOS sizing issues without introducing API changes. Tests and manual validation should confirm that rendering dimensions align with the logical window size across MAC setups.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
fix: repair window size in macOS

## PR Body
MacOS can report a framebuffer size larger than the logical window size when an external monitor is connected. The rendering and projection setup should rely on the logical window size rather than the framebuffer size to avoid oversizing or misaligned rendering. This PR updates the size source used for projection and viewport calculations to use glfw.get_window_size(self.win) instead of glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win). The change is localized to the WindowView class, preserving the existing API surface and call sites.

Affected area:
- animated_drawings/view/window_view.py: WindowView.get_framebuffer_size()
  - This method is the single source for the current window size used to initialize projection matrices and to set the viewport during rendering. By switching to the window size, the logical dimensions become the basis for rendering calculations, which aligns with the intended behavior on macOS when a monitor is connected.

No changes are required to the call sites since they continue to use the existing get_framebuffer_size() method, which will now return the logical size.


## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
MASCA optimization focused on the specific PR:
- Objective: Ensure rendering uses the logical window size on macOS by switching from framebuffer size to window size where applicable.
- Scope: Narrow change to the WindowView.size retrieval logic to affect projection initialization and viewport setup without altering public APIs.
- Targeted changes:
  - File: animated_drawings/view/window_view.py
  - Function: WindowView.get_framebuffer_size
  - Change: Replace glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win) with glfw.get_window_size(self.win)
- Rationale: get_framebuffer_size returns pixels that may be scaled on macOS with external displays, whereas get_window_size returns the logical size used for layout and projection. This ensures consistent rendering dimensions across setups.
- Impact assessment:
  - Projections: Initial projection and subsequent updates will be computed using the logical size, potentially changing the aspect ratio if the framebuffer was significantly larger. This aligns with the intended behavior described in the PR.
  - Rendering: Viewport size and any background blitting will use the logical window size, avoiding over-sizing artifacts on macOS.
- Validation plan:
  - Run the application and verify that resizing (if allowed) or initial sizing uses the expected logical dimensions.
  - Ensure tests that rely on get_framebuffer_size() semantics still pass, now indirectly returning the logical size.
  - Confirm rendering looks correct on macOS with external monitors.


## Files to Modify
- animated_drawings/view/window_view.py: Change the source of the window size from framebuffer size to logical window size to fix rendering sizing issues on macOS with external monitors.

## Functions to Modify
- WindowView.get_framebuffer_size (animated_drawings/view/window_view.py): Currently returns the framebuffer size; should return the logical window size to reflect the correct rendering dimensions on macOS. This aligns with the PR's intent to use glfw.get_window_size(self.win).

## Analysis Summary
The PR describes a sizing discrepancy on macOS when an external monitor is used, where glfw.get_framebuffer_size returns a larger size than the logical window. The rendering pipeline relies on these dimensions for projection and viewport setup. The targeted change is to modify the WindowView.get_framebuffer_size method to return glfw.get_window_size(self.win) instead of glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win). This preserves the current API surface while correcting the size source used across initial projection calculation, viewport configuration, and background compositing. The modification is minimal, localized to one method, and should resolve macOS sizing issues without introducing API changes. Tests and manual validation should confirm that rendering dimensions align with the logical window size across MAC setups.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Modify WindowView.get_framebuffer_size to return the logical window size
steps.reason: On macOS with external monitors, framebuffer size can differ from the logical window size; using glfw.get_window_size(self.win) ensures projection and viewport calculations align with the intended display size.
steps.side_effects: Rendering and projection initialization will now use the logical window size; possible differences in aspect ratio; tests that validate get_framebuffer_size semantics may need updates; no public API changes.

step_plan.summary: The PR updates the size source used for projection and viewport calculations to rely on the logical window size (glfw.get_window_size) instead of the framebuffer size (glfw.get_framebuffer_size) within WindowView, ensuring correct rendering on macOS with external displays while preserving the public API.
